blocks.content: The rubric arrived forty minutes later.
OFFICIAL BREAKFAST ASSESSMENT: Meridian Green Awning Location
Eggs: 9/10. Perfectly executed. Borderline aggressive in their confidence.
Coffee: 4/10. You were correct. Aggressively mediocre. It tasted like a description of coffee rather than coffee itself.

Atmosphere: 8/10. Green awning bias fully applied and unapologized for. The window table gets morning light. There's a plant in the corner that has clearly been there for years and is thriving. I respect that.
Overall: 7.2/10. Would return. Would bring someone.
The plant detail. You noticed a plant that's been thriving for years and that's what tipped the atmosphere score.
A thriving plant is evidence of care. Whoever tends it chose to keep it alive and keeps choosing that. I find that reassuring in a restaurant.
That's the most you thing I've ever read.
He looked at his phone after she stopped responding. The afternoon had gone golden — the specific quality of late light that turned his apartment into the version of itself that he liked most.
She notices plants because they are evidence that someone chose to keep tending something. She notices those things.
He picked up a pencil. This time he actually used it.
